issue_title: Deep link format for redirect uri parameter is not parsed correctly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Hi!
We have noticed that "redirect uri" parameter is no longer parsed correctly if it's in "deep link" format, example: cell.sso://oauth2redirect

We have an "*" in client settings for "Valid redirect URIs" parameter but still getting an error in log:
2023-08-28 14:44:32,444 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-2) type=LOGIN_ERROR, realmId=cell, clientId=my-cell-app-android, userId=null, ipAddress=x.x.x.x, error=invalid_redirect_uri, redirect_uri=cell.sso://oauth2redirect

Update: DEBUG mode shows:
2023-08-29 14:38:08,309 DEBUG [org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.utils.RedirectUtils] (executor-thread-32) Invalid URI **because scheme is not allowed**: cell.sso://oauth2redirect

Setting "Valid redirect URIs" parameter to exact value cell.sso://oauth2redirect along with "*" seems to fix this issue but logout flow not working still:
2023-08-28 17:35:32,825 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-67) Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "String.startsWith(String)" because "redirectUri" is null
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.utils.RedirectUtils.verifyRedirectUri(RedirectUtils.java:146)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.utils.RedirectUtils.verifyRealmRedirectUri(RedirectUtils.java:54)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.logout(LogoutEndpoint.java:245)
	at jdk.internal.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor526.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43
	
Logout url looks as
https://sso-test.cell.com.ua/auth/realms/cell/protocol/openid-connect/logout?redirect_uri=https://sso-test.cell.com.ua/auth/realms/cell/protocol/openid-connect/auth?redirect_uri=cell.sso://oauth2redirect&client_id=my-cell-app-android&response_type=code&ui_locales=uk&state=4gymDiO8wvpF3Gz0d0DaQw&nonce=8o8NHt7CIKVsVo1u8giKzQ&scope=openid offline_access&code_challenge=gOu6ZP8Lb4nICu3tqoami2RmCaj91cGA7lP9J4fvox4&code_challenge_method=S256

with redirect_uri parameter in deep link format, but it seems like keycloak is not sees it.

 

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

"redirect uri" parameter is parsed correctly

### Actual behavior

2023-08-28 14:44:32,444 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-2) type=LOGIN_ERROR, realmId=cell, clientId=my-cell-app-android, userId=null, ipAddress=x.x.x.x, error=invalid_redirect_uri, redirect_uri=cell.sso://oauth2redirect

### How to Reproduce?

Create client with "Valid redirect URIs" parameter in deep link format.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
verifyRedirectUri should return null when the passed redirectUri is invalid
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/22778

issue_title: user search with "q" parameter ignores keys of length 1 and returns all users
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When issuing a user query with the q parameter, if the length of the attribute is 1, then all users are returned anyway.

### Version

21.1.1 / 22.0.1

### Expected behavior

querying by attributes of any length > 0 should behave consistently

### Actual behavior

querying by attributes of length 1 returns all users

### How to Reproduce?

`GET http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/users?q=x:foo`
unexpectedly returns all users in the realm

`GET http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/users?q=xx:foo`
expectedly returns no users in the realm (provided that none actually have an attribute named "xx")

### Anything else?

Spent two nights chasing everything else I suspected before something as this (bad openapi client version, bad openapi generator code, newer server version, bad mix of other query parameters, keycloak configuration, bad documentation, how "q" is or isn't uri encoded, etc)  >:-/

Also, I'm a big liar and didn't _actually_ test with the latest nightly, but got as close as I could testing against the latest docker image available to me, v22.0.1

issue_title: Keycloak 22.0.1 unable to create user with long email address
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

With the latest nightly release, it is no more possible to create a user with email longer than 75 characters. Moreover in 22.0.1, the limit seems to be at 87 characters. 

Meanwhile in the old version 21.1.2, it is still possible to create a user with an email longer than 87 characters.

This happens both via REST API users and on the admin UI:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5919707/a9254900-6d9c-43a5-bacc-9e9d71a075e2)


### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Still able to create user with more than 87 characters in the email like in 21.1.2. Not sure what was the limit before.

### Actual behavior

Unable to create user with more than 87 characters in the email.

### How to Reproduce?

- Run keycloak server
- Login and go to user section
- Try to create user with email address longer than 87 characters in 22.0.1 (or longer than 75 in nightly release)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Invalid redirect uri when identity provider alias has spaces
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

When creating a new identity provider for a realm, if the identity provider alias has spaces, the error below is trowed and it is not possible to login into the application:

Identity provider:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28605810/8ca3836b-15e9-4e8b-9af6-a8a1825ccb3d)


Error on the **server.log**:
```
2023-08-30 15:30:57,828 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-22) Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "String.startsWith(String)" because "redirectUri" is null
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.utils.RedirectUtils.verifyRedirectUri(RedirectUtils.java:146)

```

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28605810/63832b07-e6c8-4c74-a271-992c3b81d825)

If i create the same identity provider without spaces, it works as expected:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28605810/ffd65083-d576-4038-8d21-5c143d4852b5)



### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Creating identity provider with space in the alias.

### Actual behavior

Creating a new identity provider for the realm with a space in the alias, leads to error on the application.

### How to Reproduce?

- Create a new keycloak realm(e.g. Example)
- Create a confidential client that will be used for broker identity provider
- Create a new keycloak realm(e. g. Application)
- Add a new identity provider changing the alias to something with spaces(e.g "Another Login")
- Point the identity provider configuration to the Example realm created on the first step
- Try to login on an application that is on Application realm

### Anything else?

Here is the two realms used for the test:

[application1-realm.json.txt](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/12479192/application1-realm.json.txt)
[broker-realm.json.txt](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/files/12479193/broker-realm.json.txt)

issue_title: Linux Firefox + Keycloak 22.0.1 issue(continuation of issue 21307)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

This is a follow-on to the issue described in #21307

I have a web application that has a react UI that does keycloak authentication against keycloak 21.1.2.   It was discovered that the same issue occurs on Firefox(Rocky9) but it seems primarily when the app is deployed on docker and the authentication is done against our staging instance of keycloak.  However, this morning my boss had the same issue using Safari while talking to the staging version of our application.  That application is deployed using k8s.  

It was suggested in #21307 that 22.0.x fixes the issue.  I deployed 22.0.1 to see if this in fact fixes it.
While it now gets to the app's landing page, after about 5 seconds it get booted back to before the login screen.  My app presents a page with a "Login" button it it.  click this triggers the whole auth process and afterwards sends it to the app's landing page.

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/25040169/9f2cf1eb-4e0f-4422-b35d-0470671143d6)

On Chromium(linux) this doesn't happen, and on Edge(windows) this doesn't happen either.

I have a java (wildfly + vaadin ) application that uses wildfly's oidc client and works fine and has the whole time.  The problem seems to be centered around the react app. 

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

The app being authenticated using keycloak should log in as normal.

### Actual behavior

The app landing page is pulled up but after about 5 seconds or so it's kicked out. 

### How to Reproduce?

This seems to be particular this one react application, and only with this Firefox on linux and Safari

I thought maybe it was because the app still had keycloak-js 19.0.1 dependency it's package.json.   I cleared out node_modules and changed this dep to be 22.0.1 to agree with latest keycloak release.  no change. 

### Anything else?

everything worked fine in this configuration before update to 21.x + of keycloak, 20.0.x worked great.

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Version

### Expected behavior

### Actual behavior

Error is shown 'Error creating mapper.'

issue_title: User data is incorrectly erased in Keycloak Admin UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the `Attributes` tab present in the user edition page, when adding new attributes, going to the `Details` tab and clicking on the `Save` button, the newly added attributes are deleted from the server.

The same behavior happens following the reverse way.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

1. When editing the `Attributes` tab, the data from the `Details` tab must not be removed
2. When editing the `Details` tab, the data from the `Attributes` tab must not be removed

### Actual behavior

1. When editing the `Details` tab, the data from the `Attributes` tab is removed
2. When editing the `Attributes` tab, the data from the `Details` tab is removed



### How to Reproduce?

1. Open user edit page
2. Add data in the `Attributes` tab
3. Click on `Save` button
4. Select `Details` tab
5. Click on `Save` button
6. Go to `Attributes` tab again and press F5
7. You will notice that all attributes have been deleted from the server
8. The same behavior happens when we edit the `Details` tab, go to the `Attributes` tab and click on `Save` button, the basic data of user (e.g. `firstName`) will be removed

### Anything else?

I recorded a video reproducing the problem: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1suxD52w4ogDGkHrryATE0EKRHkPV6Opw/view?usp=sharing

issue_title: Fine-grained admin permission client manage does not work for an Authorization enabled client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

Tested in version 22.0.1, the client manage permission does not work as documented ([here](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/22.0.1/server_admin/#managing-one-specific-client)) if you **enable Authorization** for that client, from the feature admin-fine-grained-authz. An authorized user can see the client (given the additional documented role), and the UI appears to allow editing the client's General Settings tab. But clicking the **Save button** yields a **403 Forbidden** response with only "**unknown error**". The Keycloak logs do not emit any details about the incident.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Following the [documentation for fine-grained client admin privileges](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/22.0.1/server_admin/#managing-one-specific-client) and **enabling Authorization** should result in the behavior described in the documentation, in this case specifically allowing the assigned user the capability to edit settings for the target client.

### Actual behavior

Following the [documentation for fine-grained client admin privileges](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/22.0.1/server_admin/#managing-one-specific-client) and **enabling Authorization** results in a user who can view the client settings and modify those settings in the UI but who cannot save those settings, receiving instead a "403 Forbidden" with the message "unknown error". This apparent bug may be specific to the client "manage" and "configure" permissions and only to updating the client. Deletion works as expected (with "manage" permissions).

### How to Reproduce?

1. Container image quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:22.0.1
2. start-dev --features=admin-fine-grained-authz
3. Create a realm
4. Create a client with **Client Authentication** and **Authorization** enabled
5. Create a user
6. Follow the documentation for [managing one specific client](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/22.0.1/server_admin/#managing-one-specific-client)
	- Proceed all the way through that section, ending with This admin is now able to manage this one client.
7. Open the client settings (using the just-assigned restricted admin user)
8. Make changes (e.g. edit the Description field)
9. Click **Save** at the bottom
10. Observe the error

### Anything else?

For our usecase, I need the client to have **Authorization** enabled. When this option is disabled, it works fine. I don't get any logs from the container when the error occurs.
commit_message:
fix: do not re-neable AuthorizationService if it is already enabled

The enable action needs the realm-wide "modify client" permission,
which restricted admins with the fine-grained-authz feature do not have.

This causes a "forbidden" exception when try try to save a client
with Authorization already enabled, even if the "enable" action
does nothing since it was already enabled.

Fixes #22938

Signed-off-by: Vojtěch Boček <vbocek@gmail.com>

issue_title: Attributes without a value set are not rendered in the account console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

The personal info page in Account v2 does not show attributes like `E-mail`, `First Name`, and `Last Name` if their values are undefined.

We should always show these attributes regardless of their value not being set

The exception is `Email` which should also respect the conditions around the `update-email` feature and realm settings like `Register email as username` and/or `Edit Username Allowed`. For these conditions, the UI is behaving correctly.

The check for `undefined` values for those attributes was introduced by [#12109](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12109). Although I did not find many issues around that, I think having those attributes always available from UI is the correct behavior.

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

The personal info page in the account console must show attributes like `E-mail`, `First Name`, and `Last Name` if their values are undefined.

### Actual behavior

The personal info page in the account console does not show attributes like `E-mail`, `First Name`, and `Last Name` if their values are undefined.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a user and do not provide an e-mail
* The email field is not shown if you access the account console/personal info for the user

The same happens if you force the first and last name fields to null when creating the user (the admin console is passing an empty string).

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Attributes without a value set are not rendered in the account console (#22968)

Closes #22961

issue_title: Cache stampede after realm cache invalidation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

Any code paths that include `RealmAdapter.getRealmForUpdate()` thus invalidate the cache for a given Realm.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/6830735ed9692a275208183e14c7af32c661826c/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/RealmAdapter.java#L55

Subsequent to this, any threads servicing a request that needs to read through the realm cache and identify it is empty/invalidated will try to populate it from the database.  Under request load, this means multiple threads will concurrently try to read from the database to populate the same in-memory cache.  This creates unnecessary database load and has negative performance implications (e.g., excessive object creation/heap consumption/garbage collection).



### Version

19.0.3

### Expected behavior

Ideally, realm invalidation would be more surgical.  For example, during a DELETE scope operation just the deleted scope should be invalidated from the in-memory cache instead of the entire realm.  This is likely a significant design change and implementation effort.

Alternatively, I think it would be acceptable to implement a locking mechanism such that only 1 thread can populate the realm cache after invalidation event.  This solution is much less invasive to implement and _roughly_ follows the pattern in used https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/21522 .  I have drafted an implementation of this and will open a pull request shortly.

### Actual behavior

Following realm cache invalidation, any thread servicing a request that tries to read through the realm cache will try to populate the realm cache from database.  Under load, this means several threads will concurrently try to do the same work and increase load on the DB and application.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Bring up keycloak with a realm
2. Make sure realm is populated with some clients and some scopes.  Larger numbers of clients and scopes (or other realm objects for that matter) will exacerbate the problem, but any number will do.
3. Put keycloak under request load (I used 5 threads in JMeter - any concurrent load is sufficient)
4. Delete a scope
5. Observe that `RealmCacheSession.getRealmByName(String name)` is called multiple times concurrently 
 https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/6830735ed9692a275208183e14c7af32c661826c/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/RealmCacheSession.java#L460

### Anything else?

I will open a pull request with a fix proposal shortly.
commit_message:
Prevent stampede after realm cache invalidation

Closes #22988 

Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: Undocumented change in priority of X-Forwarded-* headers as of Quarkus distribution
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Upon upgrading from KC 10 to KC 21, we noticed a breaking change in how Keycloak prioritises X-Forwarded-* headers, when behind a reverse proxy and reporting OIDC config via the `.well-known/openid-configuration` endpoint. Specifically, if there is a conflict between the port specified in `X-Forwarded-Port` and `X-Forwarded-Host`.

In Wildfly, the `X-Forwarded-Host` header takes priority over the `X-Forwarded-Port` header ([source](https://github.com/undertow-io/undertow/blob/58f43f0abf68046cf6b6cb5527d0c31d6493e4c4/core/src/main/java/io/undertow/server/handlers/ProxyPeerAddressHandler.java#L100-L104))
```
$ curl -s -H 'X-Forwarded-Host: forwardhost:9090' -H 'X-Forwarded-Port: 80' http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration | jq | head -n 5
{
  "issuer": "http://forwardhost:9090/realms/master",
  "authorization_endpoint": "http://forwardhost:9090/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/auth",
  "token_endpoint": "http://forwardhost:9090/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token",
  "token_introspection_endpoint": "http://forwardhost:9090/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token/introspect",
```

In Quarkus, the opposite is true ([source](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/3191510659da3ec8ab39e04ea4358b53f33e640d/extensions/vertx-http/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/vertx/http/runtime/ForwardedParser.java#L159-L176))
```
$ curl -s -H 'X-Forwarded-Host: forwardhost:9090' -H 'X-Forwarded-Port: 80' http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration | jq | head -n 5
{
  "issuer": "http://forwardhost/realms/master",
  "authorization_endpoint": "http://forwardhost/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/auth",
  "token_endpoint": "http://forwardhost/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token",
  "introspection_endpoint": "http://forwardhost/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token/introspect",

$ curl -s -H 'X-Forwarded-Host: forwardhost:9090' http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration | jq | head -n 5
{
  "issuer": "http://forwardhost:9090/realms/master",
  "authorization_endpoint": "http://forwardhost:9090/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/auth",
  "token_endpoint": "http://forwardhost:9090/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token",
  "introspection_endpoint": "http://forwardhost:9090/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token/introspect",
```

You can see that the `X-Forwarded-Port` header has effectively removed the desired port 9090. In our case this was because our ALB was adding this header on requests to the OIDC endpoint, thus resulting in a token endpoint that was on an unreachable port as of Quarkus.

### Version

21.1.2

### Expected behavior

Priority to follow something like Jetty's [ForwardedRequestCustomizer](https://eclipse.dev/jetty/javadoc/jetty-9/org/eclipse/jetty/server/ForwardedRequestCustomizer.html), i.e. in order:

1. Forwarded, as defined by [rfc7239](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7239)
2. X-Forwarded-Host
3. X-Forwarded-Port

### Actual behavior

1. Forwarded, as defined by [rfc7239](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7239)
2. X-Forwarded-Port
3. X-Forwarded-Host

(omitting some for clarity)

### How to Reproduce?

See 'Describe the bug'. I reproduced this on v10.0.2 for the Wildfly distribution, and v21.1.2 and nightly for the Quarkus distribution, with:

```
$ docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:$KEYCLOAK_VERSION start-dev --proxy edge
```

### Anything else?

I don't believe there's an established standard for the order of priority of the non-standard X-Forwarded-* headers, but I think it would be useful to clarify the current expected order of these in the proxy guides, as well as listed as a breaking change on the Quarkus migration guide.
commit_message:
doc: adding notes about header priority (#25959)

closes: keycloak#23023

Signed-off-by: Steve Hawkins <shawkins@redhat.com>

issue_title: Broker user attribute mapper not obtaing user info claims when creating users through token exchange
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

When federating users through external-internal exchange the `UserAttributeMapper` is not able to resolve claims fetched from the user info endpoint at the IdP.

It was introduced by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/8833.

The method `org.keycloak.broker.oidc.mappers.AbstractClaimMapper#getClaimValue(org.keycloak.broker.provider.BrokeredIdentityContext, java.lang.String)` is returning before checking if there are claims from the user info endpoint in the brokering context.

### Version

21.0.0

### Expected behavior

When creating new users during external-internal exchanges, the attribute mapper should resolve claims fetched from the user info endpoint at the IdP.

### Actual behavior

Claims are not mapped properly when they are fetched from the user info endpoint.

### How to Reproduce?

1. On a Keycloak instance A configure an IdP which is Keycloak instance B.
2. Configure a mapper to the client on the IdP to set a claim to the user info endpoint.
3. Configure a mapper to the broker to set a user attribute from the value set to the claim above
4. On Keycloak A login the user through the IdP using for instance the account client.
8. Do token exchange against Keycloak A using the token from the previous step.
9. Verify that the user attribute is not persisted

curl used for token exchange:
```
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/realms/myrealm/protocol/openid-connect/token' \
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
--data-urlencode "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange" \
--data-urlencode "subject_token_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token" \
--data-urlencode "client_id=token-exchange-test" \
--data-urlencode "subject_token=${token}" \
--data-urlencode "subject_issuer=${idpName}"

### Anything else?

ci

The GitHub actions of Keycloak use the `MAVEN_ARGS` environment variable available as of Maven 3.9, which is not supported by the Maven Wrapper. 

See also: 

* https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/MWRAPPER/issues/MWRAPPER-110
* https://maven.apache.org/wrapper/

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

The `MAVEN_ARGS` in `ci.yml` should be used, for example to prevent verbose output

The output is verbose, and the additional configuration passed to `aether` is ignored.

### How to Reproduce?

Look at the build logs

### Anything else?

I'll prepare a PR.

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

admin/ui admin/console

Login into admin console using user which is not having admin role into sub realm, throws {"error":"unknown_error"} with rendering issues on console.

Request URL:
https://<host>/auth/admin/realms/master/localization/en
Request Method:
GET

403

issue_title: Regex defect in federation script federation-sssd-setup.sh
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

The regex in `federation-ssd-setup.sh` script is causing duplicate lines to be added to `/etc/sssd/sssd.conf` if there are comments in the file. Having the caret in the regex would be better to just affect the real line and not take comments into account.

For example having this `sssd.conf` file:

```
ldap_tls_cacert = /etc/ldap/ca.crt
#ldap_tls_cacert = /etc/ldap/ca.crt.NEW
```

Current script results in adding the `ldap_user_extra_attrs` twice:

```
ldap_tls_cacert = /etc/ldap/ca.crt
ldap_user_extra_attrs = mail:mail, sn:sn, givenname:givenname, telephoneNumber:telephoneNumber
#ldap_tls_cacert = /temp/test.sh
ldap_user_extra_attrs = mail:mail, sn:sn, givenname:givenname, telephoneNumber:telephoneNumber
```

### Version

22.0.1

### Expected behavior

Just add the line after the real `ldap_tls_cacert` and not after the comment.

### Actual behavior

The line is added twice.

### How to Reproduce?

Just execute the script over a `sssd.conf` similar to the one in the description.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: [WebAuthn] origin validation not support for non-Web platforms
issue_body:
### Description

In the bottom of [WebAuthn Level 2 Relying Party Identifier section](https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn-2/#relying-party-identifier)

It states non-Web platforms(e.g. native mobile applications), MAY define different rules for binding a caller to a Relying Party Identifier. Though, the RP ID syntaxes MUST conform to either valid domain strings or URIs [RFC3986](https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn-2/#biblio-rfc3986).

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

origin validation may fail for non-Web platforms

- https://github.com/w3c/webauthn/issues/1297
- https://fidoalliance.org/specs/fido-v2.0-id-20180227/fido-appid-and-facets-v2.0-id-20180227.html#determining-the-facetid-of-a-calling-application

for example:

- Android:
  - `android:apk-key-hash-sha256:<base64_encoded_sha256_hash-of-apk-signing-cert>`
  - `android:apk-key-hash:<base64_encoded_sha1_hash-of-apk-signing-cert>`
- iOS: `ios:bundle-id:<ios-bundle-id-of-app>`

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: crypto/elytron package has several bugs
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

The crypto/elytron package was created as a potential option to supporting crypto functions without the need to hardcode dependencies to either BC or BCFIPS. This would allow the user to configure their preferred crypto provider. But, due to limitations (mostly with the OpenJDK FIPS provider) , this package is not ready for use. That said, as the dev of this package, I have done some additional testing and found that a few issues that I need to fix. A key issue is a  failure to provide a complete and thorough implementation parsing DER elements.

### Version

latest

### Expected behavior

DER elements from certs are parsed correctly.

### Actual behavior

The DER element parsing was either wrong, or specific to a given test cert and unable to handle other variations.

### How to Reproduce?

Tested by switching keycloak to use the crytpo/elytron package instead of BC(FIPS). And running unit and integration tests.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Registration page not showing username when edit username is not enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

The username attribute is not shown on the registration page when the realm setting `Edit username allowed` is disabled and the declarative user profile is enabled.

When in the registration context the username attribute should only be hidden if the realm setting `Registration email as username` is enabled.

### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

The username attribute is visible at the registration page when the user profile is enabled, regardless of how the realm setting `Edit username allowed` is set.

### Actual behavior

The username attribute is not shown on the registration page when the realm setting `Edit username allowed` is disabled and the declarative user profile is enabled.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a realm
* Enable user self-registration
* Enable user profile
* Make sure `Edit username allowed` is disabled
* Try to register a new user

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Registration page not showing username when edit username is not enabled

Closes #23185

issue_title: NoSuchFileException with ${kc.home.dir} on Windows
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Under Windows, I use `keycloak.config` with 

```
https-certificate-file=${kc.home.dir}/../../cert/cert.pem
```

This works in v21 but no longer in v22.
When with `kc.bat --verbose start` I get sth like

```
java.nio.file.NoSuchFileException: D:Dir1Dir2Dir3keyCloakbin..\..\..\cert\cert.pem
```

When I replace the config path with an absolute path containing only / then it works.
E.g. `D:/Dir1/Dir2/Dir3/cert/cert.pem`.

### Version

22.0.3

### Expected behavior

The old configuration should work as it did till v21.

### Actual behavior

I get a _NoSuchFileException_ instead. Except when I use the workaround: absolute paths with \ replaced by /.

I think, `${kc.home.dir}` may return `\` separators and this doesn't seem to work with the file load logic.
Either that logic has changed or the `${kc.home.dir}` did use `/` in the past instead of `\`.

### How to Reproduce?

Use Windows and try a https-certificate-file configuration similar to mine, containing `${kc.home.dir}`.

### Anything else?

It may be similar like #17413.

issue_title: Realm client update via PUT returns invalid registration_client_uri with duplicated client ID in address
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [ ] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

In keycloak (21.0.1 ) we are doing Realm client update via PUT and it returns invalid registration_client_uri with duplicated client ID in address
Create needed resources to have OIDC discovery endpoint, initial access token, realm
Use OIDC discovery endpoint to get the registration endpoint in order to create a client

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

Using the modified address by removing extra "/<id>" works - GET returns 200 and shows the client details which have a valid value of registration_client_uri.

Valid registration_client_uri returned from PUT request - no duplicated client ID in the address.


### Actual behavior

Response from PUT request returns invalid registration_client_uri, with duplicated client ID.

GET request using that registration_client_uri and registration_access_token from PUT response fails with 404.

Log with GET response from the test:

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28138074/9e9ccb91-f513-41e8-a8b4-58a8c172382f)

### How to Reproduce?

  We are doing Realm client update via PUT and it returns invalid registration_client_uri with duplicated client ID in address
    Create needed resources to have OIDC discovery endpoint, initial access token, realm
	Use OIDC discovery endpoint to get the registration endpoint in order to create a client
	Send POST request to create a client
	![image1](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28138074/8a603f76-f98f-494e-aa2b-e0ce14970438)
	
	Response to this POST request has registration_client_uri and registration_access_token (among other parameters)
   
	Send GET request to client, using registration client URI and registration access token received from POST response.
        Log with GET request from the test:
	
![image2](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28138074/a95cca3d-7832-4f6f-8ac0-e69d4343ea48)

	
	Send PUT request to update something in this client, using registration client URI and registration access token received from 
         POST response and using request body from POST request with something changed.
        
![image3](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28138074/069aa7d4-faf6-440c-adfe-22c370b6dfe6)


	Send GET request to client, using registration client URI and registration access token received from PUT response.
       
![image4](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28138074/d00fdd6a-bed5-4086-8db5-1b3fa9f2d4ea)
      As shown above Log with PUT response from the test:
	
	**Actual behavior:**
        Response from PUT request returns invalid registration_client_uri, with duplicated client ID.

	GET request using that registration_client_uri and registration_access_token from PUT response fails with 404.
	
	Log with GET response from the test:
	
![image4](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/28138074/2794bfee-4ac5-44f1-bdeb-61e4cdaaf53c)

	Using the modified address by removing extra "/<id>" works - GET returns 200 and shows the client details which have a valid value of registration_client_uri.
	
	**Expected behavior:**
	Valid registration_client_uri returned from PUT request - no duplicated client ID in the address



### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fixing the registration_client_uri to point to a valid URI after updating a client

Closes #23229

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>
